name: White Chocolate Raspberry Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the crushed wafers with sugar and melted butter.
2. Press into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan. Bake at 350 degrees for 8 minutes. Let cool.
FILLING
- 2 1/2 lbs cream cheese, cubed and room temperature
- 2 cups sugar
- 1/4 cup flour
- 5 large eggs
- 1/3 cup milk
- 1/2 lb white chocolate, melted
- 1 pint fresh raspberries or 1 pkg. loose-packed unsweetened frozen raspberries (do not thaw)
1. Beat cream cheese, sugar and flour in a large bowl with a heavy-duty mixer until very smooth.
2. Beat in eggs, one at a time, only until blended.
3. Stir in milk, then white chocolate, by hand.
4. Grease sides of prepared pan and pour in half of the cheesecake mixture. Sprinkle with half of the raspberries. Repeat with remaining cheesecake batter and raspberries, pressing the second layer of raspberries down into the batter so they don't dry out in the oven.
1. Bake at 375 degrees for 15 minutes.
2. Reduce heat to 235 degrees and continue baking for 1 hour and 30 minutes or until done. The center will no longer be sticky but will not appear cooked; it will firm up upon cooling.
3. Let come to room temperature and refrigerate overnight. Remove from pan.
TOPPING
- 1/2 cup semi-sweet chocolate chips, melted
- 2 tbsp Crisco shortening
- 1/2 cup heavy cream, whipped
- 1 tbsp powdered sugar
